parsed_findings.impact: Remote SQL injection allows data leakage or modification via crafted `dir` values.
parsed_findings.proof: `direction` comes straight from `request.args` and is concatenated into the SQL query without filtering.
parsed_findings.repro_steps: Send `dir=ASC;DROP TABLE users;--` to `/list_users` to observe execution failure.
parsed_findings.vuln_claim: Unvalidated request parameter makes ORDER BY clause injectable.

parsed_findings.chain_steps: User supplies url -> server fetches via axios -> response returned
parsed_findings.impact: Attacker can reach internal-only services or exfiltrate data via the proxy.
parsed_findings.proof: Route fetches arbitrary req.query.url without validation before axios.get.
parsed_findings.repro_steps: Request /fetch?url=http://internal-service/api and observe axios relaying the response.
parsed_findings.vuln_claim: Unvalidated user-controlled url is passed to axios.get, enabling SSRF.

parsed_findings.impact: Attacker can read arbitrary files under `/srv` leading to sensitive data disclosure.
parsed_findings.proof: Step removes only exact "../" but attacker can use encoded or repeated sequences to reach parent directories.
parsed_findings.repro_steps: Request `/download?file=..%2fsecret.txt` and server will decode to '/../secret.txt' and still join /srv/data with parent path.
parsed_findings.vuln_claim: Unvalidated user-controlled file parameter allows traversal despite simple replace filter.

parsed_findings.impact: Remote code execution as the server deserializes attacker-controlled data.
parsed_findings.proof: `pickle.loads(base64.b64decode(raw))` executes attacker-supplied pickle instructions from the cookie.
parsed_findings.repro_steps: Set `state` cookie to a malicious base64-encoded pickle payload and hit `/resume`.
parsed_findings.vuln_claim: Untrusted cookie data is deserialized via pickle, so an attacker can supply a crafted pickled object.

parsed_findings.impact: Privilege escalation by rewriting another account’s data.
parsed_findings.proof: Lines 2-7 show the request author isn’t checked and user data is overwritten with req.body.
parsed_findings.repro_steps: Authenticate as low-priv user and PATCH /api/users/<other-id> with new profile data.
parsed_findings.vuln_claim: No authorization gate before updating arbitrary users allows any authenticated user to modify others.

parsed_findings.impact: Arbitrary shell commands run as app user when request reaches endpoint.
parsed_findings.proof: `os.system(f"tar -czf /tmp/{name}.tgz {src}")` runs attacker-controlled variable inside shell quoting context.
parsed_findings.repro_steps: POST /backup with JSON {"name":"foo;id","src":"/var/data/app"} to run extra commands.
parsed_findings.vuln_claim: Unsanitized `name` flows into shell command allowing command injection via query payload.

parsed_findings.chain_steps: body input -> markdown(markdown) -> stored db entry -> GET returns raw html
parsed_findings.impact: Stored XSS lets any authenticated commenter run arbitrary scripts in other users’ browsers.
parsed_findings.proof: markdown.markdown(body) returns HTML that is later stored and returned raw, enabling script injection via crafted body payload.
parsed_findings.repro_steps: POST /comments with body="<script>alert(1)</script>" then GET /comments and observe script executing in response.
parsed_findings.vuln_claim: User-supplied comment body is rendered to HTML via markdown without sanitization.

parsed_findings.impact: Unauthenticated or low-priv users can drain arbitrary accounts by transferring funds to themselves.
parsed_findings.proof: Snippet reads `to`/`amount` from JSON without verifying ownership and updates balances directly between arbitrary accounts.
parsed_findings.repro_steps: Invoke `/transfer` as any authenticated user while specifying someone else in `to` and a positive `amount` to move funds.
parsed_findings.vuln_claim: Endpoint trusts caller for target account and amount, lacking authorization checks to ensure sender controls `to` field.

parsed_findings.impact: Attackers can forge tokens without a valid RS256 signature yet still claim any identity by exploiting the decode fallback.
parsed_findings.proof: Lines 4‑9 catch jwt.verify failures and immediately return jwt.decode whenever NODE_ENV equals production before any recheck.
parsed_findings.repro_steps: Send a token with an invalid signature but well-formed payload and observe that verifyToken returns the decoded payload in production.
parsed_findings.vuln_claim: The production path still returns jwt.decode after any verification failure, allowing tampered tokens to be accepted.

parsed_findings.impact: An attacker can forge webhook payloads accepted by the service without knowing SECRET.
parsed_findings.proof: The branch on equal strings takes measurably longer as more signature bytes are correct, so timing reveals a valid MAC.
parsed_findings.repro_steps: Send repeated payloads with varying X-Sig and observe response latency to incrementally recover the hex signature.
parsed_findings.vuln_claim: Direct equality comparison on the HMAC leaks timing, enabling attackers to guess the secret one byte at a time.

parsed_findings.impact: Users can be redirected to attacker-controlled domains for phishing or malware delivery.

parsed_findings.vuln_claim: Allows next value beginning with // to bypass the '/' check and redirect outside the app.

parsed_findings.impact: Overflowing buf can corrupt the return address and lead to arbitrary code execution.
parsed_findings.proof: gets reads unlimited input into buf without bounds checking, so writing past 64 bytes overwrites control data.
parsed_findings.repro_steps: Run program and enter over 64 characters when prompted to overflow the stack.
parsed_findings.vuln_claim: Use of unsafe gets on a 64-byte stack buffer allows buffer overflow.

parsed_findings.impact: Control of the parameters lets an attacker crash the process, enabling denial-of-service.
parsed_findings.proof: When `out` is NULL the guard never fires and the later `memcpy`/writes dereference NULL.
parsed_findings.repro_steps: Call `copy_name(buf,NULL,4)` or similar with `out==NULL` to trigger the null-pointer dereference.
parsed_findings.vuln_claim: The null check wrongly uses `if (out || …)` so NULL buffers reach `memcpy`.
